title: How Machines Actually Learn

modules.title: From Data to Decision: The Core Loop
modules.sub: Tracing how raw data becomes a model's verdict
modules.description: Learners map out the full machine learning pipeline — from collecting and labelling training data through to a model outputting a decision — using the three flawed systems as live examples. They identify what choices were made at each stage and flag which ones could already be causing harm. By the end, they can sketch the training, validation, and testing cycle from memory.

modules.title: When the Model Gets It Dangerously Wrong
modules.sub: Investigating real decisions that hurt real people
modules.description: Learners dig into case files for the hiring tool, medical screener, and content filter to find the moment each model produced a harmful output. They classify the type of failure — false positive, false negative, or confidence miscalibration — and record their findings in an auditor's log. This module builds the vocabulary and analytical habit they will need for the final verdict.

modules.title: Overfitting, Bias, and Other Hidden Traps
modules.sub: Naming the invisible problems buried inside models
modules.description: Learners are introduced to at least three named failure modes — including overfitting, dataset bias, and label leakage — and match each one to evidence already spotted in the case files. They run structured comparison tasks that show how a model can perform well on test data yet still behave unfairly in the real world. The goal is to move from noticing that something is wrong to being able to name and explain exactly why.

modules.title: Audit a Real Model: Your Verdict Matters
modules.sub: Writing an evidence-based verdict that calls out failures
modules.description: Learners consolidate everything by producing a written audit verdict on one of the three model scenarios, drawing on their logs and failure-mode analysis from earlier modules. They must state what went wrong, name the failure modes responsible, and recommend whether the model should be paused, fixed, or retired. The verdict is structured like a real professional document, giving learners a tangible output they can critique and defend.

synopsis: You're an AI auditor called in after three different machine learning systems — a hiring tool, a medical screener, and a content filter — have made decisions that hurt real people. You have to dig into the data and training choices to find out exactly why.
rationale: Teens who understand the mechanics of how models are trained — and where training systematically fails — are far better equipped to question AI outputs they will encounter as voters, workers, and consumers.
learning_outcome: Students can explain the training, validation, and testing cycle of a machine learning model, identify at least three failure modes by name, and produce a written audit verdict on a given model scenario.
